schemas package¶

Pydantic schemas for request/response models.

Each domain has its own submodule (e.g. schemas/health.py). Schemas must never import from models/, services/, or routers/.

Submodules¶

schemas.admin_metrics module¶

Pydantic schemas for the admin metrics dashboard.

All response models provide aggregate data suitable for a real-time admin frontend — combining DB counts and Prometheus-backed latency/error metrics.

class schemas.admin_metrics.EpisodeStats(*, added_total=0, added_24h=0, in_progress=0, enrichment_pending=0, fully_enriched=0, with_embeddings=0, fully_enriched_pct=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Episode metrics — ingestion pipeline status.

Parameters:

  • added_total (int)

  • added_24h (int)

  • in_progress (int)

  • enrichment_pending (int)

  • fully_enriched (int)

  • with_embeddings (int)

  • fully_enriched_pct (float)

added_24h: int¶
added_total: int¶
enrichment_pending: int¶
fully_enriched: int¶
fully_enriched_pct: float¶
in_progress: int¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

with_embeddings: int¶

Bases: BaseModel

Graph entity metrics.

Parameters:

  • entities_total (int)

  • entities_24h (int)

  • relationships_total (int)

entities_24h: int¶
entities_total: int¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

relationships_total: int¶

class schemas.admin_metrics.MetricsSummaryResponse(*, episodes=<factory>, graphs=<factory>, users_total=0, request_rate=<factory>, error_rate_pct=0.0, overall_latency_ms=<factory>, context_latency_ms=<factory>, graph_search_latency_ms=<factory>, queue_depth=None, total_requests=0, active_requests=0, status='ok', message=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Aggregated metrics for the admin dashboard frontend.

Combines DB counts with Prometheus-sourced latency and error metrics. The status field indicates whether Prometheus is reachable.

Parameters:

  • episodes (EpisodeStats)

  • graphs (GraphStats)

  • users_total (int)

  • request_rate (dict[str, float])

  • error_rate_pct (float)

  • overall_latency_ms (LatencyPercentiles)

  • context_latency_ms (LatencyPercentiles)

  • graph_search_latency_ms (LatencyPercentiles)

  • queue_depth (QueueDepth | None)

  • total_requests (int)

  • active_requests (int)

  • status (str)

  • message (str | None)

active_requests: int¶
context_latency_ms: LatencyPercentiles¶
episodes: EpisodeStats¶
error_rate_pct: float¶
graph_search_latency_ms: LatencyPercentiles¶
graphs: GraphStats¶
message: str | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

overall_latency_ms: LatencyPercentiles¶
queue_depth: QueueDepth | None¶
request_rate: dict[str, float]¶
status: str¶
total_requests: int¶
users_total: int¶

schemas.admin_stats module¶

Pydantic schemas for admin dashboard statistics.

All response models aggregate data across an entire organization.

class schemas.admin_stats.OrgStatsResponse(*, organization_id, total_users, total_sessions, total_episodes, total_facts, total_messages, total_api_keys)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Aggregate statistics for the dashboard overview.

Provides a quick snapshot of the organization’s data volume across all users and sessions.

Parameters:

  • organization_id (UUID)

  • total_users (int)

  • total_sessions (int)

  • total_episodes (int)

  • total_facts (int)

  • total_messages (int)

  • total_api_keys (int)

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

organization_id: UUID¶
total_api_keys: int¶
total_episodes: int¶
total_facts: int¶
total_messages: int¶
total_sessions: int¶
total_users: int¶
class schemas.admin_stats.UsageStatsResponse(*, date, message_count, session_count)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Daily usage statistics for the dashboard.

Parameters:

  • date (str)

  • message_count (int)

  • session_count (int)

date¶

The date (YYYY-MM-DD) for this data point.

Type:

str

message_count¶

Number of messages processed on this date.

Type:

int

session_count¶

Number of sessions created on this date.

Type:

int

message_count: int¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

session_count: int¶

schemas.api_keys module¶

Pydantic schemas for project-scoped API key management.

Used by project settings for listing, creating, and revoking API keys scoped to a specific project. The raw key value is returned exactly once at creation time.

class schemas.api_keys.ApiKeyCreatedResponse(*, id, name, prefix, project_id, created_by=None, scopes, is_revoked, last_used_at=None, created_at, raw_key, message='Save this API key — it will not be shown again.')[source]¶

Bases: ApiKeyResponse

Response for key creation — includes the raw key (shown once).

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • name (str)

  • prefix (str)

  • project_id (UUID)

  • created_by (UUID | None)

  • scopes (list[str])

  • is_revoked (bool)

  • last_used_at (datetime | None)

  • created_at (datetime)

  • raw_key (str)

  • message (str)

raw_key¶

The full API key string. Shown only once — not retrievable later.

Type:

str

message¶

Warning to save the key.

Type:

str

message: str¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

raw_key: str¶

Paginated response for GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/api-keys.

  • total (int)

data: list[ApiKeyResponse]¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

total: int¶
class schemas.api_keys.ApiKeyResponse(*, id, name, prefix, project_id, created_by=None, scopes, is_revoked, last_used_at=None, created_at, raw_key=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response body for a single API key.

The raw_key field is only populated on creation and is None in list/get responses.

  • id (UUID)

  • name (str)

  • prefix (str)

  • project_id (UUID)

  • created_by (UUID | None)

  • scopes (list[str])

  • is_revoked (bool)

  • last_used_at (datetime | None)

  • created_at (datetime)

  • raw_key (str | None)

created_at: datetime¶
created_by: UUID | None¶
id: UUID¶
is_revoked: bool¶
last_used_at: datetime | None¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶
prefix: str¶
project_id: UUID¶
raw_key: str | None¶
scopes: list[str]¶

schemas.audit_log module¶

schemas.auth module¶

Pydantic schemas for dashboard authentication.

Covers signup, login, token refresh, and the token response format. All auth schemas are request/response models — never stored or logged directly.

class schemas.auth.DashboardUserResponse(*, id, email, name=None, role='member', organization_id, is_email_verified=False, mfa_enabled=False)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Public-facing dashboard user profile.

Returned by user-info endpoints — never includes the password hash.

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • email (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • role (str)

  • organization_id (UUID)

  • is_email_verified (bool)

  • mfa_enabled (bool)

email: str¶
id: UUID¶
is_email_verified: bool¶
mfa_enabled: bool¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str | None¶
organization_id: UUID¶
role: str¶
class schemas.auth.LoginRequest(*, email, password)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/auth/login.

Parameters:

  • email (EmailStr)

  • password (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1)])

email: EmailStr¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

password: str¶
class schemas.auth.LoginResponse(*, access_token=None, refresh_token=None, expires_in=None, token_type=None, requires_mfa=False, mfa_session_token=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Unified response for POST /v1/auth/login.

When MFA is disabled, returns JWT tokens as normal. When MFA is enabled, returns requires_mfa=true with a mfa_session_token for the second step.

Parameters:

  • access_token (str | None)

  • refresh_token (str | None)

  • expires_in (int | None)

  • token_type (str | None)

  • requires_mfa (bool)

  • mfa_session_token (str | None)

access_token: str | None¶
expires_in: int | None¶
mfa_session_token: str | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

refresh_token: str | None¶
requires_mfa: bool¶
token_type: str | None¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/auth/mfa/disable.

Parameters:

  • password (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1)])

  • otp (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=4), MaxLen(max_length=8)])

otp: str¶
password: str¶

class schemas.auth.SignupRequest(*, email, password, organization_name)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/auth/signup.

Creates a new organization with an admin dashboard user.

Parameters:

  • email (EmailStr)

  • password (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=8), MaxLen(max_length=128)])

  • organization_name (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255)])

email: EmailStr¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

organization_name: str¶
password: str¶
class schemas.auth.SignupResponse(*, message, email)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response body for POST /v1/auth/signup.

Instead of returning tokens directly (the user must first verify their email), signup returns a confirmation message. The client should then call POST /v1/auth/verify-email with the OTP received via email.

Parameters:

  • message (str)

  • email (EmailStr)

email: EmailStr¶
message: str¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class schemas.auth.TokenResponse(*, access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, token_type='Bearer')[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response body for login and refresh endpoints.

Parameters:

  • access_token (str)

  • refresh_token (str)

  • expires_in (int)

  • token_type (str)

access_token¶

Short-lived JWT for API authentication.

Type:

str

refresh_token¶

Long-lived token for session renewal.

Type:

str

expires_in¶

Access token TTL in seconds.

Type:

int

token_type¶

Always "Bearer".

Type:

str

access_token: str¶
expires_in: int¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

refresh_token: str¶
token_type: str¶
class schemas.auth.UpdateProfileRequest(*, name=None, email=None, current_password=None, new_password=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for PATCH /v1/auth/me.

All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated. To change the password, provide both current_password and new_password.

Parameters:

  • name (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=512)])

  • email (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=320)])

  • current_password (Annotated[str | None, MinLen(min_length=1)])

  • new_password (Annotated[str | None, MinLen(min_length=8), MaxLen(max_length=128)])

current_password: str | None¶
email: str | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str | None¶
new_password: str | None¶
class schemas.auth.VerifyEmailRequest(*, email, otp)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/auth/verify-email.

The OTP is a 6-digit code received via email. On success, returns a TokenResponse so the user is immediately authenticated.

Parameters:

  • email (EmailStr)

  • otp (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=4), MaxLen(max_length=8)])

email: EmailStr¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

otp: str¶

schemas.classifications module¶

Pydantic schemas for dialog classification query responses.

class schemas.classifications.ClassificationResponse(*, id, episode_id, intent=None, emotion=None, valence=None, arousal=None, confidence, created_at)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Classification result for a single episode.

Returned by the classification query endpoint. Excludes the raw LLM output field — that is available via direct DB access if needed.

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • episode_id (UUID)

  • intent (str | None)

  • emotion (str | None)

  • valence (str | None)

  • arousal (str | None)

  • confidence (float)

  • created_at (datetime)

arousal: str | None¶
confidence: float¶
created_at: datetime¶
emotion: str | None¶
episode_id: UUID¶
id: UUID¶
intent: str | None¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

valence: str | None¶

schemas.common module¶

Common Pydantic schemas used across multiple domains.

Includes: - PaginatedResponse[T] — generic wrapper for cursor / offset pagination. - ErrorResponse — RFC 7807 Problem Details structure. - ValidationErrorDetail — per-field validation error.

class schemas.common.ErrorResponse(*, type, title, status, detail, instance=None, request_id=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

RFC 7807 Problem Details response body.

See https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7807 for the specification.

Parameters:

  • type (str)

  • title (str)

  • status (int)

  • detail (str)

  • instance (str | None)

  • request_id (str | None)

type¶

A URI that identifies the problem type.

Type:

str

title¶

A short, human-readable summary.

Type:

str

status¶

The HTTP status code.

Type:

int

detail¶

A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence.

Type:

str

instance¶

A URI that identifies the specific occurrence (optional).

Type:

str | None

request_id¶

The correlation ID for this request (optional).

Type:

str | None

detail: str¶
instance: str | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

request_id: str | None¶
status: int¶
title: str¶
type: str¶
class schemas.common.PaginatedResponse(*, data, next_cursor=None, has_more=False, total=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel, Generic[T]

Generic paginated response wrapper.

Use as the response model for list endpoints:

class ItemResponse(BaseModel): …

@router.get(“/items”, response_model=PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]) async def list_items(…) -> PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]: …

Parameters:

  • data (list[T])

  • next_cursor (str | None)

  • has_more (bool)

  • total (int | None)

data¶

The page of results.

Type:

list[T]

next_cursor¶

Opaque cursor for the next page (cursor-based pagination).

Type:

str | None

has_more¶

True when there are additional pages beyond this one.

Type:

bool

total¶

Total number of matching records across all pages (optional).

Type:

int | None

data: list[T]¶
has_more: bool¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

next_cursor: str | None¶
total: int | None¶
class schemas.common.PaginatedResponse(*, data, next_cursor=None, has_more=False, total=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel, Generic[T]

Generic paginated response wrapper.

Use as the response model for list endpoints:

class ItemResponse(BaseModel): …

@router.get(“/items”, response_model=PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]) async def list_items(…) -> PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]: …

Parameters:

  • data (list[T])

  • next_cursor (str | None)

  • has_more (bool)

  • total (int | None)

data¶

The page of results.

Type:

list[T]

next_cursor¶

Opaque cursor for the next page (cursor-based pagination).

Type:

str | None

has_more¶

True when there are additional pages beyond this one.

Type:

bool

total¶

Total number of matching records across all pages (optional).

Type:

int | None

data: list[T]¶
has_more: bool¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

next_cursor: str | None¶
total: int | None¶
class schemas.common.PaginatedResponse(*, data, next_cursor=None, has_more=False, total=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel, Generic[T]

Generic paginated response wrapper.

Use as the response model for list endpoints:

class ItemResponse(BaseModel): …

@router.get(“/items”, response_model=PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]) async def list_items(…) -> PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]: …

Parameters:

  • data (list[T])

  • next_cursor (str | None)

  • has_more (bool)

  • total (int | None)

data¶

The page of results.

Type:

list[T]

next_cursor¶

Opaque cursor for the next page (cursor-based pagination).

Type:

str | None

has_more¶

True when there are additional pages beyond this one.

Type:

bool

total¶

Total number of matching records across all pages (optional).

Type:

int | None

data: list[T]¶
has_more: bool¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

next_cursor: str | None¶
total: int | None¶
class schemas.common.PaginatedResponse(*, data, next_cursor=None, has_more=False, total=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel, Generic[T]

Generic paginated response wrapper.

Use as the response model for list endpoints:

class ItemResponse(BaseModel): …

@router.get(“/items”, response_model=PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]) async def list_items(…) -> PaginatedResponse[ItemResponse]: …

Parameters:

  • data (list[T])

  • next_cursor (str | None)

  • has_more (bool)

  • total (int | None)

data¶

The page of results.

Type:

list[T]

next_cursor¶

Opaque cursor for the next page (cursor-based pagination).

Type:

str | None

has_more¶

True when there are additional pages beyond this one.

Type:

bool

total¶

Total number of matching records across all pages (optional).

Type:

int | None

data: list[T]¶
has_more: bool¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

next_cursor: str | None¶
total: int | None¶
class schemas.common.SearchResponse(*, results, total, query=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Unified search response from a cross-source search.

Parameters:

  • results (list[SearchResultItem])

  • total (int)

  • query (str | None)

results¶

Ranked list of search results (episodes + facts merged and sorted by descending score).

Type:

list[SearchResultItem]

total¶

Total number of results returned.

Type:

int

query¶

The original search query (echoed back for client reference).

Type:

str | None

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

query: str | None¶
results: list[SearchResultItem]¶
total: int¶
class schemas.common.SearchResultItem(*, id, type, content, score, metadata=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single search result item from a unified search.

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • type (str)

  • content (str)

  • score (float)

  • metadata (dict | None)

id¶

The source record UUID.

Type:

str

type¶

Source type — "episode" or "fact".

Type:

str

content¶

The text content.

Type:

str

score¶

Relevance score (higher = more relevant).

Type:

float

metadata¶

Additional type-specific fields (role for episodes, subject/predicate/object/confidence for facts).

Type:

dict | None

content: str¶
id: str¶
metadata: dict | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

score: float¶
type: str¶
class schemas.common.ValidationErrorDetail(*, loc, msg, type)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Per-field validation error detail.

Mirrors the structure returned by FastAPI’s default RequestValidationError handler.

Parameters:

  • loc (list[str | int])

  • msg (str)

  • type (str)

loc¶

Location path (e.g. ["body", "email"]).

Type:

list[str | int]

msg¶

Human-readable error message.

Type:

str

type¶

Machine-readable error type (e.g. "value_error.missing").

Type:

str

loc: list[str | int]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

msg: str¶
type: str¶

schemas.context module¶

Pydantic schemas for the context assembly domain.

Includes request validation and response models for the context assembly endpoint. Schemas must never import from models/, services/, or routers/.

class schemas.context.ContextMetadata(*, cache_hit=False, assembly_time_ms=0.0, source_counts=<factory>, total_items=0)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Metadata returned alongside the assembled context block.

Parameters:

  • cache_hit (bool)

  • assembly_time_ms (float)

  • source_counts (dict[str, Any])

  • total_items (int)

cache_hit¶

Whether the result was served from cache.

Type:

bool

assembly_time_ms¶

Wall-clock time for context assembly (ms).

Type:

float

source_counts¶

Breakdown of items by source type.

Type:

dict[str, Any]

total_items¶

Total number of items included in the context.

Type:

int

assembly_time_ms: float¶
cache_hit: bool¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

source_counts: dict[str, Any]¶
total_items: int¶
class schemas.context.ContextRequest(*, query, limit=20, format='text')[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Query parameters for GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/context.

Parameters:

  • query (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=2000)])

  • limit (Annotated[int, Ge(ge=1), Le(le=100)])

  • format (Annotated[str, _PydanticGeneralMetadata(pattern='^(text|json)$')])

query¶

Natural-language query to retrieve relevant context for.

Type:

str

limit¶

Maximum number of items per source type to include (1–100).

Type:

int

format¶

Output format — "text" for plain text or "json" for structured JSON.

Type:

str

format: str¶
limit: int¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

query: str¶
class schemas.context.ContextResponse(*, context, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response body for the context assembly endpoint.

Parameters:

  • context (str)

  • metadata (ContextMetadata)

context¶

The assembled context block as a string (text or JSON serialised, depending on the requested format).

Type:

str

metadata¶

Assembly metadata including cache status, timing, and source breakdown.

Type:

ContextMetadata

context: str¶
metadata: ContextMetadata¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

schemas.custom_instructions module¶

Pydantic schemas for organization-level custom instructions.

Custom instructions are named domain-specific text snippets that guide extraction behavior. They are scoped by (organization_id, scope, target_id) where scope is one of extraction or user_summary.

Each instruction has a human-readable name (e.g. “legal_domain”) and the text content that is injected into the extraction prompt.

class schemas.custom_instructions.CustomInstructionSchema(*, name, text)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single named custom instruction.

Parameters:

  • name (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255)])

  • text (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1)])

name¶

Human-readable label (e.g. “legal_domain”, “healthcare”).

Type:

str

text¶

The instruction text injected into the extraction prompt.

Type:

str

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶
text: str¶
class schemas.custom_instructions.CustomInstructionsResponse(*, data)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response wrapper for listing custom instructions.

Parameters:

data (list[CustomInstructionSchema])

data: list[CustomInstructionSchema]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class schemas.custom_instructions.SetCustomInstructionsRequest(*, instructions)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request to replace all custom instructions for a scope.

The existing instructions for the scope are replaced atomically.

Parameters:

instructions (Annotated[list[CustomInstructionSchema], MaxLen(max_length=50)])

instructions: list[CustomInstructionSchema]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

schemas.extraction_schemas module¶

Pydantic schemas for extraction schema CRUD operations.

Schemas define the JSON Schema contracts an organization uses for structured extractions (type='structured') or classification label sets (type='classification').

class schemas.extraction_schemas.CreateExtractionSchemaRequest(*, name, json_schema, type='structured', prompt_template=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for creating a new extraction schema.

Parameters:

  • name (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255), _PydanticGeneralMetadata(pattern='^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_\\- ]*$')])

  • json_schema (dict)

  • type (Annotated[str, _PydanticGeneralMetadata(pattern='^(structured|classification)$')])

  • prompt_template (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=10000)])

name¶

Human-readable name, unique within the organization. Must start with a letter and contain only alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen, or space characters.

Type:

str

json_schema¶

For type='structured', a valid JSON Schema document. For type='classification', a dict with keys intent, emotion, valence, arousal each containing a list of allowed label strings.

Type:

dict

type¶

Schema type — 'structured' (default) or 'classification'.

Type:

str

prompt_template¶

Optional per-schema prompt template override.

Type:

str | None

json_schema: dict¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶
prompt_template: str | None¶
type: str¶
class schemas.extraction_schemas.ExtractionSchemaListResponse(*, data, total)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response model for listing extraction schemas.

Parameters:

  • data (list[ExtractionSchemaResponse])

  • total (int)

data: list[ExtractionSchemaResponse]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

total: int¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response model for a single extraction schema.

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • organization_id (UUID)

  • name (str)

  • type (str)

  • json_schema (dict)

  • prompt_template (str | None)

  • is_active (bool)

  • created_at (datetime)

  • updated_at (datetime)

created_at: datetime¶
id: UUID¶
is_active: bool¶
json_schema: dict¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶
organization_id: UUID¶
prompt_template: str | None¶
type: str¶
updated_at: datetime¶
class schemas.extraction_schemas.UpdateExtractionSchemaRequest(*, name=None, json_schema=None, prompt_template=None, is_active=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for updating an existing extraction schema.

All fields are optional. The type field is immutable after creation.

Parameters:

  • name (Annotated[str | None, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255), _PydanticGeneralMetadata(pattern='^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_\\- ]*$')])

  • json_schema (dict | None)

  • prompt_template (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=10000)])

  • is_active (bool | None)

is_active: bool | None¶
json_schema: dict | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str | None¶
prompt_template: str | None¶

schemas.facts module¶

Pydantic schemas for the facts (business data ingestion) domain.

Schemas must never import from models/, services/, or routers/.

class schemas.facts.FactBatchRequest(*, session_id=None, facts)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/facts.

Parameters:

  • session_id (str | None)

  • facts (Annotated[list[FactTriple], MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=500)])

session_id¶

Optional session external ID to associate facts with. If omitted, facts are not linked to any session.

Type:

str | None

facts¶

List of fact triples. Must contain at least 1 and at most 500 triples.

Type:

list[FactTriple]

facts: list[FactTriple]¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

session_id: str | None¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response returned after successful fact batch ingestion.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • accepted_count (Annotated[int, Ge(ge=0)])

  • status (str)

  • message (str)

job_id¶

UUID string identifying the async enrichment job.

Type:

str

accepted_count¶

Number of facts accepted for processing.

Type:

int

status¶

Always "accepted" for synchronous acknowledgement.

Type:

str

message¶

Human-readable status message.

Type:

str

accepted_count: int¶
job_id: str¶
message: str¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

status: str¶
class schemas.facts.FactResponse(*, id, content, subject=None, predicate=None, object=None, confidence=1.0, source_episode_id=None, subject_type='literal', object_type='literal', subject_entity_id=None, object_entity_id=None, created_at)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single extracted fact, returned from list endpoints.

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • content (str)

  • subject (str | None)

  • predicate (str | None)

  • object (str | None)

  • confidence (Annotated[float, Ge(ge=0.0), Le(le=1.0)])

  • source_episode_id (UUID | None)

  • subject_type (str)

  • object_type (str)

  • subject_entity_id (UUID | None)

  • object_entity_id (UUID | None)

  • created_at (datetime)

id¶

Internal fact UUID.

Type:

UUID

content¶

Human-readable fact statement.

Type:

str

subject¶

Subject entity name.

Type:

str | None

predicate¶

Relationship verb.

Type:

str | None

object¶

Object entity name.

Type:

str | None

confidence¶

Extraction confidence score (0.0–1.0).

Type:

float

source_episode_id¶

Optional FK to the source episode.

Type:

UUID | None

subject_type¶

Entity type of the subject ("literal" or "entity" when resolved to graph_entities).

Type:

str

object_type¶

Entity type of the object ("literal" or "entity" when resolved to graph_entities).

Type:

str

subject_entity_id¶

FK to graph_entities if the subject was resolved during extraction.

Type:

UUID | None

object_entity_id¶

FK to graph_entities if the object was resolved during extraction.

Type:

UUID | None

created_at¶

Fact creation timestamp.

Type:

datetime

confidence: float¶
content: str¶
created_at: datetime¶
id: UUID¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

object: str | None¶
object_entity_id: UUID | None¶
object_type: str¶
predicate: str | None¶
source_episode_id: UUID | None¶
subject: str | None¶
subject_entity_id: UUID | None¶
subject_type: str¶
class schemas.facts.FactTriple(*, subject, predicate, object, content=None, confidence=1.0)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single fact triple for batch ingestion.

Parameters:

  • subject (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=500)])

  • predicate (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=200)])

  • object (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=500)])

  • content (str | None)

  • confidence (Annotated[float, Ge(ge=0.0), Le(le=1.0)])

subject¶

The subject entity name (e.g. "Alice").

Type:

str

predicate¶

The relationship verb (e.g. "likes", "works_at").

Type:

str

object¶

The object entity name (e.g. "hiking", "Acme Corp").

Type:

str

content¶

Optional human-readable fact statement. Auto-generated from subject-predicate-object if omitted.

Type:

str | None

confidence¶

Extraction confidence score (0.0–1.0). Defaults to 1.0.

Type:

float

confidence: float¶
content: str | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

object: str¶
predicate: str¶
subject: str¶
classmethod validate_confidence(v)[source]¶

Ensure confidence is in the valid range (0.0–1.0).

Parameters:

v (float)

Return type:

float

schemas.health module¶

Pydantic schemas for health-check endpoints.

Corresponds to the /v1/health and /v1/ready routes defined in routers/health.py.

class schemas.health.HealthResponse(*, status, service='openzync-api')[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response body for the liveness probe (GET /v1/health).

Parameters:

  • status (str)

  • service (str)

status¶

Always "ok" when the service process is alive.

Type:

str

service¶

Service name identifier ("openzync-api").

Type:

str

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

service: str¶
status: str¶
class schemas.health.ReadinessResponse(*, status, checks)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response body for the readiness probe (GET /v1/ready).

Parameters:

  • status (str)

  • checks (dict[str, bool])

status¶

"ok" when all checks pass, "degraded" otherwise.

Type:

str

checks¶

Per-dependency health result (True / False).

Type:

dict[str, bool]

checks: dict[str, bool]¶
model_config = {}¶

status: str¶

schemas.llm_outputs module¶

Pydantic models representing raw LLM output contracts.

These models define the expected output shape that the LLM must produce for each extraction task. They are distinct from the API response schemas (in the same package) which include DB-generated fields (id, created_at, …) — these models contain only the fields the LLM is asked to emit.

Every model is designed to be passed as response_model to core.llm.LLMBackend.chat(), which will: * auto-inject the model’s JSON schema into the system prompt, * validate the response against the model, * retry with error context on failure.

Usage:

from schemas.llm_outputs import ClassificationOutput

response = await backend.chat(
    messages,
    response_model=ClassificationOutput,
    temperature=0.0,
)
# response.content is guaranteed valid JSON matching ClassificationOutput
data = ClassificationOutput.model_validate_json(response.content)
class schemas.llm_outputs.ClassificationOutput(*, intent=None, emotion=None, valence=None, arousal=None, confidence=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Expected LLM output for dialog classification.

All fields are optional — the LLM may choose not to classify a dimension if the input is ambiguous. The worker applies further label validation against the org’s configured allowed sets.

Parameters:

  • intent (str | None)

  • emotion (str | None)

  • valence (str | None)

  • arousal (str | None)

  • confidence (float)

arousal: str | None¶
confidence: float¶
emotion: str | None¶
intent: str | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

valence: str | None¶
class schemas.llm_outputs.CombinedLLMOutput(*, classification=<factory>, entities=<factory>, relationships=<factory>, facts=<factory>, structured_extractions=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Single LLM response combining all episode enrichment tasks.

Used by the enrich_episode worker to produce all enrichment outputs in a single LLM call instead of 4 separate calls. Each field maps to one of the previously independent LLM responses.

Parameters:

  • classification (ClassificationOutput)

  • entities (list[EntityOutput])

  • relationships (list[RelationshipOutput])

  • facts (list[FactOutput])

  • structured_extractions (dict[str, Any])

classification: ClassificationOutput¶

Classification result (intent, emotion, valence, arousal).

entities: list[EntityOutput]¶

Named entities extracted from the episode.

facts: list[FactOutput]¶

Factual knowledge triples with confidence scores.

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

relationships: list[RelationshipOutput]¶

Directed subject-predicate-object triples between entities.

structured_extractions: dict[str, Any]¶

Org-defined structured extractions keyed by schema name.

class schemas.llm_outputs.EntityExtractionOutput(*, entities=[], relationships=[])[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Expected LLM output for entity extraction.

The LLM must emit a JSON object with entities and relationships arrays (both may be empty).

Parameters:

  • entities (list[EntityOutput])

  • relationships (list[RelationshipOutput])

entities: list[EntityOutput]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

relationships: list[RelationshipOutput]¶

A single entity extracted from a conversation turn.

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • type (str)

  • summary (str | None)

model_config = {}¶

name: str¶
summary: str | None¶

class schemas.llm_outputs.FactExtractionOutput(*, facts=[])[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Expected LLM output for fact extraction.

The LLM must emit a JSON object with a facts array. Wrapping the array in an object ensures consistent Pydantic validation and avoids ambiguous top-level arrays.

Parameters:

facts (list[FactOutput])

facts: list[FactOutput]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class schemas.llm_outputs.RelationshipOutput(*, subject, predicate, object)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A directed relationship between two entities.

Field names match the subject/predicate/object convention used in all extraction prompt templates (not source/target/relation).

Parameters:

  • subject (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1)])

  • predicate (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1)])

  • object (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1)])

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

classmethod must_not_be_empty(v)[source]¶
Parameters:

v (str)

Return type:

str

object: str¶
predicate: str¶
subject: str¶
class schemas.llm_outputs.StructuredExtractionOutput(**extra_data)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Expected LLM output for structured data extraction.

Accepts any JSON object keys — the shape is defined by the org’s configured extraction schemas, which vary per deployment. This model ensures the LLM returned a valid JSON object; further schema-level validation is performed by the worker via _validate_against_schema.

Parameters:

extra_data (Any)

model_config = {'extra': 'allow'}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

schemas.mappers module¶

ORM-to-dict mapper helpers for session and episode models.

These helpers bridge the metadata_ → metadata naming convention: SQLAlchemy reserves metadata for its own DeclarativeBase, so the ORM attribute is metadata_. The Pydantic schemas use metadata. These functions convert ORM objects to plain dicts with the correct key names.

Placed in the schemas layer because they shape ORM data into the exact structure expected by Pydantic response schemas — a data-shaping concern, not a data-access concern. The service layer calls these to prepare ORM objects for model_validate.

schemas.mappers.episode_to_dict(episode)[source]¶

Convert an Episode ORM model to a flat dict for MessageResponse.

Handles the metadata_ → metadata field-name mapping for episode messages.

Parameters:

episode (Episode)

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

schemas.mappers.session_to_dict(session, *, message_count=0, fact_count=0, pending_enrichment_count=0)[source]¶

Convert a Session ORM model to a flat dict for schema construction.

Handles the metadata_ → metadata field-name mapping.

Parameters:

  • session (Session) – The Session ORM instance.

  • message_count (int) – Total number of messages in the session.

  • fact_count (int) – Total number of extracted facts.

  • pending_enrichment_count (int) – Number of messages pending enrichment.

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

schemas.mappers.session_to_list_dict(session, *, message_count=0, fact_count=0)[source]¶

Convert a Session ORM model to a lightweight list-item dict.

Matches the SessionListResponse schema (excludes metadata and updated_at for compact list responses).

Parameters:

  • session (Session)

  • message_count (int)

  • fact_count (int)

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

schemas.memory module¶

Pydantic schemas for the memory (message ingestion) domain.

Schemas must never import from models/, services/, or routers/.

class schemas.memory.Message(*, role, content, created_at=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single conversation turn within a session.

Parameters:

  • role (Annotated[str, _PydanticGeneralMetadata(pattern='^(user|assistant|system|tool)$')])

  • content (Annotated[str, MaxLen(max_length=65536)])

  • created_at (datetime | None)

  • metadata (dict[str, Any])

role¶

Message sender role — one of user, assistant, system, tool.

Type:

str

content¶

Message body text. Maximum 64KB when UTF-8 encoded.

Type:

str

created_at¶

ISO-8601 timestamp. Assigned server-side if omitted.

Type:

datetime | None

metadata¶

Optional caller-defined metadata (tags, labels, etc.).

Type:

dict[str, Any]

classmethod check_byte_size(v)[source]¶

Enforce 64KB wire-level limit per SEC-09.

max_length on the Field only checks Unicode code-point count. Multi-byte characters (e.g. emoji) can blow past 64KB on the wire while staying under the character limit. This validator catches that.

Parameters:

v (str) – The content string to validate.

Returns:

The content string unchanged if valid.

Raises:

ValueError – If the UTF-8 encoded content exceeds 65536 bytes.

Return type:

str

content: str¶
created_at: datetime | None¶
metadata: dict[str, Any]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

role: str¶

schemas.organization_config module¶

Pydantic schemas for per-organization configuration.

All settings that were previously env-var-only (Groups A, B, C) are now storable in the organizations.config JSONB column and exposed via UI.

Key pattern: - OrgConfigBase — the raw DB shape (all fields optional). - UpdateOrgConfigRequest — API input for partial updates.

class schemas.organization_config.OrgConfigBase(*, llm_backend=None, llm_model=None, llm_temperature=None, llm_max_tokens=None, openai_api_key=None, openrouter_api_key=None, azure_openai_endpoint=None, azure_openai_key=None, anthropic_api_key=None, ollama_base_url=None, embedding_backend=None, embedding_model=None, embedding_dim=None, graph_backend='surrealdb', graph_search_type=None, graph_max_traversal_depth=None, surrealdb_url=None, surrealdb_user=None, surrealdb_pass=None, surrealdb_namespace=None, surrealdb_database=None, context_cache_ttl=None, audit_log_response_body=None, reranker_backend=None, reranker_model=None, reranker_top_k=None, reranker_top_n=None, cohere_api_key=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Raw per-org config stored in the organizations.config JSONB column.

Every field is optional. When a field is None (absent from the JSONB), the caller must decide what to do — there is no env-var fallback at this layer.

Parameters:

  • llm_backend (str | None)

  • llm_model (str | None)

  • llm_temperature (Annotated[float | None, Ge(ge=0.0), Le(le=2.0)])

  • llm_max_tokens (Annotated[int | None, Ge(ge=1)])

  • openai_api_key (str | None)

  • openrouter_api_key (str | None)

  • azure_openai_endpoint (str | None)

  • azure_openai_key (str | None)

  • anthropic_api_key (str | None)

  • ollama_base_url (str | None)

  • embedding_backend (str | None)

  • embedding_model (str | None)

  • embedding_dim (Annotated[int | None, Ge(ge=64), Le(le=4096)])

  • graph_backend (str | None)

  • graph_search_type (str | None)

  • graph_max_traversal_depth (Annotated[int | None, Ge(ge=1), Le(le=10)])

  • surrealdb_url (str | None)

  • surrealdb_user (str | None)

  • surrealdb_pass (str | None)

  • surrealdb_namespace (str | None)

  • surrealdb_database (str | None)

  • context_cache_ttl (Annotated[int | None, Ge(ge=1)])

  • audit_log_response_body (bool | None)

  • reranker_backend (str | None)

  • reranker_model (str | None)

  • reranker_top_k (Annotated[int | None, Ge(ge=10), Le(le=200)])

  • reranker_top_n (Annotated[int | None, Ge(ge=1), Le(le=100)])

  • cohere_api_key (str | None)

audit_log_response_body: bool | None¶
azure_openai_endpoint: str | None¶
azure_openai_key: str | None¶
cohere_api_key: str | None¶

embedding_backend: str | None¶

surrealdb_url: str | None¶
surrealdb_user: str | None¶
to_embedding_config_dict()[source]¶

Return embedding config as a flat dict.

Only non-None fields are included. Used by worker tasks that read embedding settings directly.

Return type:

dict[str, str | int]

to_llm_config_dict()[source]¶

Return config as a dict suitable for core.llm.resolve_backend().

Only non-None fields are included. The returned dict maps our canonical field names to the provider-specific keys that _create_backend() in core/llm.py expects.

Return type:

dict[str, str | float | int]

class schemas.organization_config.OrgConfigResponse(*, stored)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response for config GET endpoints.

Returns the raw stored config. There is no env-merged effective layer — the stored config is the source of truth.

Parameters:

stored (OrgConfigBase)

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

stored: OrgConfigBase¶
class schemas.organization_config.UpdateOrgConfigRequest(*, llm_backend=None, llm_model=None, llm_temperature=None, llm_max_tokens=None, openai_api_key=None, openrouter_api_key=None, azure_openai_endpoint=None, azure_openai_key=None, anthropic_api_key=None, ollama_base_url=None, embedding_backend=None, embedding_model=None, embedding_dim=None, graph_backend=None, graph_search_type=None, graph_max_traversal_depth=None, surrealdb_url=None, surrealdb_user=None, surrealdb_pass=None, surrealdb_namespace=None, surrealdb_database=None, context_cache_ttl=None, audit_log_response_body=None, reranker_backend=None, reranker_model=None, reranker_top_k=None, reranker_top_n=None, cohere_api_key=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for PATCH /admin/organizations/{org_id}/config.

Same shape as OrgConfigBase — every field is optional, only provided fields are updated. Set a field to null to remove it (the caller will receive None for that field on the next read).

Parameters:

  • llm_backend (str | None)

  • llm_model (str | None)

  • llm_temperature (Annotated[float | None, Ge(ge=0.0), Le(le=2.0)])

  • azure_openai_key (str | None)

  • embedding_backend (str | None)

  • embedding_model (str | None)

  • graph_backend (str | None)

  • graph_search_type (str | None)

  • graph_max_traversal_depth (Annotated[int | None, Ge(ge=1), Le(le=10)])

  • surrealdb_url (str | None)

schemas.organizations module¶

Pydantic schemas for organization management.

Used by the admin bootstrap flow (POST /admin/organizations).

schemas.pii module¶

Pydantic schemas for PII detection configuration and statistics.

Stored per-org in organizations.quotas JSONB under the "pii" key. Schemas must never import from models/, services/, or routers/.

class schemas.pii.PIIConfig(*, mode=PIIMode.OFF, enabled_types=<factory>, min_confidence=0.7, sensitivity='medium')[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Per-org PII configuration, stored in organizations.quotas JSONB.

Example stored value:

{
  "pii": {
    "mode": "mask",
    "enabled_types": ["email", "phone", "ssn", "credit_card"],
    "min_confidence": 0.7,
    "sensitivity": "medium"
  }
}
Parameters:

  • mode (PIIMode)

  • enabled_types (list[str])

  • min_confidence (Annotated[float, Ge(ge=0.0), Le(le=1.0)])

  • sensitivity (Annotated[str, _PydanticGeneralMetadata(pattern='^(low|medium|high)$')])

mode¶

Processing mode — off, mask, or block.

Type:

PIIMode

enabled_types¶

Which PII types to scan for. Defaults to all regex-supported types.

Type:

list[str]

min_confidence¶

Minimum detection confidence threshold (0.0 to 1.0). Detections below this threshold are discarded.

Type:

float

sensitivity¶

Detection depth — low (regex only), medium (regex + NER), high (regex + NER + LLM fallback).

Type:

str

enabled_types: list[str]¶
min_confidence: float¶
mode: PIIMode¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

sensitivity: str¶
class schemas.pii.PIIMode(*values)[source]¶

Bases: str, Enum

PII processing modes for an organization.

off

No PII detection is performed.

mask

Detected PII is replaced with [REDACTED:{type}] placeholders.

block

Messages containing PII are rejected with a ValidationError.

BLOCK = 'block'¶
MASK = 'mask'¶
OFF = 'off'¶
class schemas.pii.PIIStats(*, detections_count=0, types_found=[], action_taken='none', duration_ms=0.0)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

PII detection statistics for a single message processing.

Parameters:

  • detections_count (int)

  • types_found (list[str])

  • action_taken (str)

  • duration_ms (float)

detections_count¶

Number of PII instances detected.

Type:

int

types_found¶

Ordered list of unique PII types found (sorted).

Type:

list[str]

action_taken¶

What action was taken — "none", "masked", or "blocked".

Type:

str

duration_ms¶

Time taken for detection + redaction in milliseconds.

Type:

float

action_taken: str¶
detections_count: int¶
duration_ms: float¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

types_found: list[str]¶

schemas.projects module¶

Pydantic schemas for project and project-member CRUD operations.

Corresponds to the /v1/projects and /v1/projects/{project_id}/members endpoints. Schemas must never import from models/, services/, or routers/.

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/members.

Parameters:

  • role (Literal['owner', 'member'])

user_id¶

The UUID of the user to add to the project.

Type:

UUID

role¶

Project role — owner or member. Defaults to "member" if omitted.

Type:

ProjectRole

model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

role: ProjectRole¶
user_id: UUID¶
class schemas.projects.CreateProjectRequest(*, name, description=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/projects.

Parameters:

  • name (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255)])

  • description (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=2000)])

  • metadata (dict[str, Any])

name¶

Human-readable project name. Must be unique within the organization. 1–255 characters.

Type:

str

description¶

Optional longer description of the project’s purpose.

Type:

str | None

metadata¶

Optional key-value metadata for extensible configuration.

Type:

dict[str, Any]

description: str | None¶
metadata: dict[str, Any]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶

Bases: BaseModel

Lightweight project representation for list endpoints.

Omits metadata and description to keep list responses compact. Use the individual GET /v1/projects/{project_id} endpoint for full details.

  • id (UUID)

  • name (str)

  • is_archived (bool)

  • updated_at (datetime)

UUID

name¶

str

is_archived¶

bool

member_count¶

int

created_by¶

UUID of the creating user, or None.

UUID | None

created_at¶

Project creation timestamp (UTC).

datetime

updated_at¶

datetime

class schemas.projects.ProjectResponse(*, id, name, description=None, metadata_=<factory>, is_archived=False, member_count=0, created_by=None, created_at, updated_at)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Full project representation returned from single-item endpoints.

Includes computed member_count populated by the service layer. Use ProjectListResponse for compact list views.

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • name (str)

  • description (str | None)

  • metadata_ (dict[str, Any])

  • is_archived (bool)

  • member_count (Annotated[int, Ge(ge=0)])

  • created_by (UUID | None)

  • created_at (datetime)

  • updated_at (datetime)

id¶

Project UUID.

Type:

UUID

name¶

Human-readable project name.

Type:

str

description¶

Optional project description.

Type:

str | None

metadata¶

Key-value metadata (maps from the ORM’s metadata_ attribute via from_attributes).

Type:

dict[str, Any]

is_archived¶

Whether the project is archived (soft-hidden).

Type:

bool

member_count¶

Number of members in this project (computed).

Type:

int

created_by¶

UUID of the user who created the project, or None if the creator was deleted.

Type:

UUID | None

created_at¶

Project creation timestamp (UTC).

Type:

datetime

updated_at¶

Last modification timestamp (UTC).

Type:

datetime

created_at: datetime¶
created_by: UUID | None¶
description: str | None¶
id: UUID¶
is_archived: bool¶
member_count: int¶
metadata: dict[str, Any]¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶
updated_at: datetime¶
schemas.projects.ProjectRole¶

Valid project membership roles.

  • owner — can manage project settings and members.

  • member — read/write access to project data.

alias of Literal[‘owner’, ‘member’]

class schemas.projects.UpdateProjectRequest(*, name=None, description=None, metadata=None, is_archived=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for PUT /v1/projects/{project_id}.

All fields are optional (PATCH semantics). Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields retain their existing values.

Parameters:

  • name (Annotated[str | None, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255)])

  • description (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=2000)])

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None)

  • is_archived (bool | None)

name¶

New project name (1–255 chars, unique within org).

Type:

str | None

description¶

New description. Set to None to clear.

Type:

str | None

metadata¶

New metadata. Deep-merged with existing metadata when provided as a partial dict.

Type:

dict[str, Any] | None

is_archived¶

Set True to archive the project, False to un-archive. Archived projects are hidden from default listing but data is preserved.

Type:

bool | None

description: str | None¶
is_archived: bool | None¶
metadata: dict[str, Any] | None¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str | None¶

class schemas.prompt_templates.PromptTemplateDetail(*, name, version, template_text, description, type=None, is_active, is_default_for_type=False, is_system_default=False)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Full template detail including the prompt text.

Used for version detail endpoints and version history. The is_system_default field is derived from the ORM model’s @property — it’s True when the template is the active system default (organization_id IS NULL and is_active).

Parameters:

  • name (str)

  • version (int)

  • template_text (str)

  • description (str | None)

  • type (str | None)

  • is_active (bool)

  • is_default_for_type (bool)

  • is_system_default (bool)

description: str | None¶
is_active: bool¶
is_default_for_type: bool¶
is_system_default: bool¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶
template_text: str¶
type: str | None¶
version: int¶

schemas.sessions module¶

Pydantic schemas for session and message CRUD operations.

Corresponds to the /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions endpoints. Schemas must never import from models/, services/, or routers/.

class schemas.sessions.CreateSessionRequest(*, external_id, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/projects/{project_id}/sessions.

Parameters:

  • external_id (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255)])

  • metadata (dict[str, Any])

external_id¶

Caller-defined session identifier. Must be unique per project (enforced by a DB unique constraint on (project_id, external_id)).

Type:

str

metadata¶

Optional metadata key-value pairs. Deep-merged on subsequent PATCH operations (not yet implemented).

Type:

dict[str, Any]

external_id: str¶
metadata: dict[str, Any]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class schemas.sessions.MessageResponse(*, id, role, content, metadata_=<factory>, token_count=0, sequence_number, created_at)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single message (episode) within a session.

Messages are ordered by sequence_number within a session for deterministic ordering (not by created_at, which can have ties).

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • role (str)

  • content (str)

  • metadata_ (dict[str, Any])

  • token_count (int)

  • sequence_number (int)

  • created_at (datetime)

content: str¶
created_at: datetime¶
id: UUID¶
metadata: dict[str, Any]¶
model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

role: str¶
sequence_number: int¶
token_count: int¶

schemas.user_summary module¶

Pydantic schemas for user summary API.

The summary is an auto-generated synopsis of a user’s conversation history, updated asynchronously by a background worker. These schemas cover the read endpoint and the trigger-regeneration endpoint.

class schemas.user_summary.UserSummaryResponse(*, user_id, summary, updated_at)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response model for fetching a user’s summary.

Parameters:

  • user_id (UUID)

  • summary (str | None)

  • updated_at (datetime | None)

user_id¶

The internal OpenZync user UUID.

Type:

UUID

summary¶

The generated summary text, or None if not yet generated.

Type:

str | None

updated_at¶

When the summary was last regenerated, or None.

Type:

datetime | None

model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

summary: str | None¶
updated_at: datetime | None¶
user_id: UUID¶
class schemas.user_summary.UserSummaryTriggerResponse(*, message, status='processing', user_id)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response model for triggering a summary regeneration.

Parameters:

  • message (str)

  • status (str)

  • user_id (UUID)

message¶

Human-readable confirmation message.

Type:

str

status¶

Job status indicator — always "processing" on initial acceptance.

Type:

str

user_id¶

The internal OpenZync user UUID.

Type:

UUID

message: str¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

status: str¶
user_id: UUID¶

schemas.users module¶

Pydantic schemas for the User domain.

All request/response models for user CRUD endpoints live here. Response models use from_attributes = True for ORM-to-schema conversion.

class schemas.users.CreateUserRequest(*, external_id, name=None, email=None, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for POST /v1/users.

Parameters:

  • external_id (Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=255)])

  • name (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=512)])

  • email (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=320)])

  • metadata (dict[str, Any])

external_id¶

Caller-defined unique user identifier. Must be unique within the organization.

Type:

str

name¶

Optional human-readable display name.

Type:

str | None

email¶

Optional email address (validated to contain @).

Type:

str | None

metadata¶

Arbitrary JSON metadata for the user. Deep-merged on update.

Type:

dict[str, Any]

email: str | None¶
external_id: str¶
metadata: dict[str, Any]¶

v (str | None)

Return type:

str | None

classmethod validate_external_id(v)[source]¶

Validate external_id does not exceed 255 bytes when UTF-8 encoded.

Parameters:

v (str)

Return type:

str

class schemas.users.UpdateUserRequest(*, name=None, email=None, metadata=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Request body for PATCH /v1/users/{user_id}.

All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated. metadata is deep-merged into existing metadata, not replaced.

Parameters:

  • name (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=512)])

  • email (Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=320)])

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None)

name¶

New display name. Set to null to clear.

Type:

str | None

email¶

New email address. Set to null to clear.

Type:

str | None

metadata¶

Metadata keys to merge into existing metadata. Set a key to null to remove it. Does not replace the full metadata dict.

Type:

dict[str, Any] | None

email: str | None¶

v (str | None)

str | None

dict[str, Any]

datetime

datetime

class schemas.users.UserResponseWithStats(*, id, external_id, name=None, email=None, metadata=<factory>, organization_id, created_at, updated_at, is_deleted=False, message_count=0, fact_count=0, session_count=0)[source]¶

Bases: UserResponse

Extended user response with aggregate statistics.

Inherits all fields from UserResponse and adds summary counts for ingested messages, extracted facts, and sessions.

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • external_id (str)

  • name (str | None)

  • email (str | None)

  • metadata (dict[str, Any])

  • organization_id (UUID)

  • created_at (datetime)

  • updated_at (datetime)

  • is_deleted (bool)

  • message_count (int)

  • fact_count (int)

  • session_count (int)

message_count¶

Total number of ingested messages (episodes).

Type:

int

fact_count¶

Total number of extracted facts for this user.

Type:

int

session_count¶

Total number of sessions (including closed).

Type:

int

fact_count: int¶

Public representation of a webhook endpoint.

Never exposes the signing secret — that is shown once on creation.

  • id (UUID)

  • organization_id (UUID)

  • name (str)

  • url (str)

  • events (list[str])

  • created_at (datetime)

  • updated_at (datetime)

created_at: datetime¶
events: list[str]¶
id: UUID¶
is_active: bool¶

model_config = {'from_attributes': True}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

organization_id: UUID¶
updated_at: datetime¶
url: str¶
class schemas.webhook.WebhookEventCategoriesResponse(*, categories)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

All event types grouped by category for the UI.

Parameters:

categories (dict[str, list[WebhookEventTypeResponse]])

categories: dict[str, list[WebhookEventTypeResponse]]¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class schemas.webhook.WebhookEventTypeResponse(*, type, label, category, description)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Describes a single subscribable event type.

Parameters:

  • type (str)

  • label (str)

  • category (str)

  • description (str)

category: str¶
description: str¶
label: str¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

type: str¶
class schemas.webhook.WebhookSecretResponse(*, id, name, url, secret, message='This is the global webhook signing secret. Use it to verify HMAC-SHA256 signatures on all received webhooks. Rotate via the OZ_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET environment variable.')[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Response returned once after creating a webhook endpoint.

The secret field is the raw signing secret and is never persisted in plaintext — the client must save it immediately.

Parameters:

  • id (UUID)

  • name (str)

  • url (str)

  • secret (str)

  • message (str)

id: UUID¶
message: str¶
model_config = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str¶
secret: str¶
url: str¶
